label: bauhaus_white_45deg
prompt: A handheld garment steamer redesigned with Bauhaus design principles, based on the real internal component stacking. The form strictly follows the functional gun-grip form factor driven by thermal engineering: the barrel section houses the boiler (widest part, approximately 90mm diameter cylinder), the water tank sits on top adding height, and the handle extends downward at 75 degrees for ergonomic vertical steaming. The soleplate at the front is a flat stainless steel rectangle. BAUHAUS TREATMENT: the barrel is a clean cylinder with flat end caps — no sculpted transitions, just pure cylinder geometry meeting flat planes. The water tank on top is a transparent rectangular box with visible water level — honest material, function exposed. The handle is a smaller cylinder intersecting the barrel at a precise 75 degree angle — the intersection is clean and geometric, not blended or filleted. Color: matte white body (RAL 9003) with ONE accent — a perfect circle vermillion red power button (∅25mm) on the barrel side. The soleplate rim is raw brushed stainless steel — material honesty. Small black sans-serif text: model number. Total dimensions approximately 130x130x300mm, weight ~1kg — a real producible product. Three-quarter hero shot, 45-degree angle. Pure white studio background. Professional product photography, photorealistic, 8K quality.

label: bauhaus_white_front
prompt: A handheld garment steamer with Bauhaus design, front view. Real proportions based on internal stacking: barrel section ~90mm wide (houses boiler), water tank visible on top, handle below at 75 degrees. The barrel is a pure cylinder. Water tank is a transparent rectangular box sitting on top. Handle is a smaller cylinder. Flat stainless steel soleplate visible at the front with geometric grid of steam holes. Matte white body, one red circle power button, brushed steel soleplate. No decoration, no unnecessary curves. Straight-on front view, eye level. White background. Product photography, photorealistic, 8K.

label: bauhaus_white_side
prompt: A handheld garment steamer with Bauhaus design, side profile. The side view reveals the true L-shaped silhouette driven by thermal engineering: barrel cylinder on top-front (90mm wide, houses 150-280 degree boiler), handle cylinder extending down-back at 75 degrees (35-45mm diameter, cool zone for hand). The water tank is a transparent rectangular block sitting on top of the barrel. The angle between barrel and handle creates thermal isolation — maximum distance between hot boiler and cool grip. Matte white, red circle button, brushed steel soleplate at front. Clean cylinder-meets-cylinder intersection. Side profile, 90-degree angle. White background. Product photography, photorealistic, 8K.

label: bauhaus_section_view
prompt: Technical cross-section illustration of a Bauhaus-designed handheld garment steamer, showing all internal components in their real positions. Cutaway view revealing: transparent water tank on top with water visible, silicone feed tube going down to the die-cast aluminum boiler block in the center of the barrel (shown in metallic gray), PTC heating elements embedded in the boiler (shown in copper/orange), thermal insulation layer wrapping the boiler (light yellow), steam distribution chamber below the boiler, flat stainless steel soleplate at the front with steam holes. In the handle section: PCB control board, trigger microswitch mechanism, wiring harness, power cord exit at bottom. The outer shell is shown in semi-transparent white to reveal the internals. Technical illustration style, labeled components, clean white background, precise engineering drawing quality, photorealistic materials, 8K.

label: bauhaus_black_45deg
prompt: A handheld garment steamer with Bauhaus design, black variant. Same engineering-driven proportions: barrel cylinder (90mm, boiler inside), water tank on top (transparent so water is visible against black body — material honesty), handle at 75 degrees. Matte black body (RAL 9005). ONE accent: a perfect circle primary yellow (Bauhaus yellow) power button. Brushed dark stainless steel soleplate. Black braided power cord. The transparent water tank creates striking contrast against the black body. Severe, geometric, purposeful. 45-degree angle hero shot. White background. Product photography, photorealistic, 8K.

label: bauhaus_three_colors
prompt: Three handheld garment steamers side by side, all Bauhaus design with identical engineering-driven proportions (barrel cylinder + top water tank + angled handle). LEFT: matte white body, red circle power button, transparent water tank. CENTER: matte black body, yellow circle power button, transparent water tank showing water. RIGHT: matte warm gray body, blue circle power button, transparent water tank. All have brushed stainless steel soleplates. Three Bauhaus primary color accents on otherwise monochrome bodies. Same real proportions ~130x130x300mm. Equal spacing, catalog photography. White background, studio lighting, photorealistic, 8K.

label: bauhaus_in_use
prompt: A woman using the white Bauhaus handheld garment steamer (red circle button, transparent water tank on top, brushed steel soleplate) to steam a navy wool blazer hanging on a simple black metal rack. Her hand grips the handle naturally — wrist in neutral position because the 75-degree handle angle matches the vertical steaming motion perfectly. Steam rising from the soleplate onto the fabric. Minimalist apartment, white walls, wood floor, one framed Bauhaus typography poster visible. Morning light. The product's geometric purity fits this environment. Lifestyle photography, natural light, editorial quality, 8K, photorealistic.

label: bauhaus_detail_intersection
prompt: Close-up detail shot showing where the barrel cylinder meets the handle cylinder on the white Bauhaus garment steamer. A clean geometric intersection of two cylinders at 75 degrees — no soft fillet, no blend, just a precise meeting of two geometric primitives with a thin recessed parting line. The red circle power button is nearby. The matte white surface has a fine industrial texture. This joint is the Bauhaus design signature — honest geometry, no decoration of the structural intersection. Macro photography, studio lighting, photorealistic, 8K.
